concerns.text: Spec SC-001 (>1,000 entries) and SC-002 (inter-family generalization) require a valid data pipeline and family-based split. Tasks T013d (pipeline), T013e (volume check), and T013f (split generation) are marked as active but the Analyze Report explicitly flags them as 'REJECTED' due to missing artifacts/truncated code. No replacement tasks exist to satisfy these critical success criteria.

Spec SC-002 (MAPE < 15% on unseen families) and SC-003 (inference time) require model training and evaluation. Tasks T018b (training loop), T018c (logging), and T021a (generalization test) are marked 'REJECTED' in the Analyze Report. Without these, the model cannot be trained or evaluated against the success criteria.

Spec SC-005 requires identifying 3 descriptors with p < 0.05. Tasks T023 (SHAP p-values) and T024 (permutation p-values) are marked 'REJECTED' with missing output files. No alternative tasks are defined to perform the statistical significance testing required by SC-005.

Task T020 (k-fold cross-validation) is marked [X] (complete) but has no implementation details, output artifact, or dependency chain defined. It appears orphaned and does not clearly map to a specific success criterion or user story requirement distinct from T017/T021a.

Tasks T050, T051, and T052 duplicate the scope of T029, T034, T035, and T033 (terminology compliance). T050/T051 repeat the exact same README/methodology updates as T029/T035. T052 duplicates T032. This creates redundant work and potential conflict without a clear merge strategy.

Task T013e references `MIN_ENTRY_THRESHOLD` in `config.py` but no task exists to define this constant value or ensure it is populated in the configuration, creating a runtime dependency gap for the volume check logic.
